import org.slf4j.Logger;

// JV005 (port of ERC003): System.exit inside a catch must be preceded by a
// capture or a print of the caught, or carry a marker; otherwise the process
// dies silently on the swallowed error. Ordering matters - a capture after the
// exit does not cover it (that path already left the process).
class ExitInCatch {
    private final Logger log;

    ExitInCatch(Logger log) {
        this.log = log;
    }

    void silentExit() {
        try {
            risky();
        } catch (Exception e) { // want "JV001"
            System.exit(1); // want "JV005"
        }
    }

    void capturedThenExit() {
        try {
            risky();
        } catch (Exception e) {
            log.error("fatal", e);
            System.exit(1);
        }
    }

    void exitBeforeCapture() {
        try {
            risky();
        } catch (Exception e) {
            System.exit(1); // want "JV005"
            log.error("late", e);
        }
    }

    void markerSuppressesExit() {
        try {
            risky();
        } catch (Exception e) {
            // no-report: fail-fast shutdown, the launcher surfaces the error
            System.exit(1);
        }
    }

    void risky() throws Exception {}
}
